title: The Discrepancy Book
description: Sprocket shows the party a small notebook where he records time discrepancies. Character reveal, no plot advance.

steps.text: Sprocket had a notebook. It was small, leather-bound, worn at the corners, and he carried it in an inside pocket he did not often share.

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: I've been keeping this for fourteen years, I think. Or forty. I check, but the check itself — well.

steps.text: Three columns. Date. Expected. Observed. The expected column was tidy. The observed column was not. The margin contained a single phrase, written repeatedly in different hands — Sprocket's own, at different ages — Something is wrong.

lines.speaker: Alchemist
lines.text: These aren't random. They're systematic. There's a pattern.

lines.speaker: Alchemist
lines.text: What have you done about it?

lines.speaker: Saboteur
lines.text: Why don't you show it to the Regulator?

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: I've requested an audience. I've been on the schedule for it.

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: Eleven years. Or fourteen. I should check. The book would know.

steps.text: He put the book back in the inner pocket. The pocket, the Alchemist noted quietly, was patched from the inside. It had been patched many, many times.
